title: Ownership as Architecture — No Garbage, No Guessing
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Rust ownership model eliminates entire classes of runtime bugs at compile time with zero runtime cost
content.proof: The borrow checker enforces: exactly one owner, N immutable borrows XOR 1 mutable borrow, lifetimes must outlive their references. This is checked at compile time. No GC pause, no use-after-free, no data races. The cost is compile time, not runtime.
content.benchmark: Rust snap: ~1.8B ops/sec (vs Fortran 2.27B). The gap is SIMD autovectorization maturity, not language fundamental.
content.caveat: Learning curve is real. The borrow checker doesn't negotiate. Fight it and you lose. Understand it and you gain compile-time guarantees no other systems language offers.
